title: 4 Ingredient Matcha Marshmallows
description: These healthy 4-ingredient homemade matcha marshmallows are soft, fluffy, and refined sugar free. Easy to make and perfect for a better for you sweet treat.

hero.alt: Soft fluffy homemade matcha marshmallows dusted with matcha powder on a white surface

thumbnail.alt: 4 ingredient matcha marshmallows recipe card - easy refined sugar free dessert

instructions.text: In your stand mixer add the gelatin and 1/2 cup of water and let it bloom.
To a saucepan add the matcha, 1/2 cup of water and maple syrup. Let it heat for 5 minutes on medium heat but don't let it boil.
Turn the stand mixer on medium speed and slowly pour in the hot liquid.
Turn to high speed for 6–8 minutes until the texture begins to fluff and thicken.
Quickly pour into a lined 8 inch / 20cm square baking tray — work fast so it doesn't set in the bowl!
Refrigerate for at least 5 hours or until set.

notes: Work quickly when pouring into the tray — the mixture sets fast.
For optimal results, let set overnight in the fridge.
Ensure the maple syrup gets hot enough so the gelatin dissolves properly.

story.subtitle: soft, fluffy refined sugar free homemade treat
paragraphs: if you've ever wanted to make homemade marshmallows but thought it sounded complicated, this recipe will change your mind.
they're soft, fluffy, lightly sweet, and completely different from store-bought versions loaded with additives.

Plus these matcha marshmallows are made with healthier gut soothing ingredients — gelatin is a source of collagen and contains amino acids that help support gut lining integrity and overall digestive health.

faq.question: Are these homemade marshmallows refined sugar free?
faq.answer: Yes, these matcha marshmallows are made with maple syrup meaning they are refined sugar free and have a lower glycemic index compared to traditional marshmallows.

faq.question: Why are my homemade marshmallows not fluffy?
faq.answer: Marshmallows need to be whipped long enough to properly incorporate air. It's best to go by texture: the mixture should become thick, glossy, and significantly increased in volume before pouring into your pan.

tips: Once the marshmallows have reached whip texture, pour them quickly into the baking tray to avoid it setting in the bowl!
Let the marshmallows set overnight in the fridge for optimal results.
Ensure that the maple syrup gets hot enough so the gelatin dissolves properly.
